We reached a settlement when I honestly didn't believe it would be possible.  I am not disappointed at all and got everything we needed.  The office assistant staff were also great and getting me information and dates and helping to also help the situation be smoother.  I guess if you want a lawyer that is confident, professional and will knock their socks off while fighting like lions and doing it with grace....yeah you are in the right place. I called every family attorney in our area trying to find the best option. The Conflict Resolution Center was the first and only to make me feel heard, and like my ex could also be heard. We set our appointment, not thinking they would make it so simple. But, they did. One sit down together with our mediator is all we needed. reviews.text:
I'm the legally appointed personal representative of my dear mother's estate. RIP Mom <3  I retained this firm in late December, 2019, after learning my dear mother had died.  I was my mother's legal POA, medical POA, best friend and caregiver before estranged family interfered in Maryland and took my very sick mother to Utah, under the cover of lies, where she'd never lived. She had documented Alzheimer's dementia and was already in a safe memory care center in Maryland.

I spoke with a lawyer at this firm telling her the whole truth. I paid the required retainer. Then I emailed important information she'd requested. I didn't hear back for one week, then two weeks; so I called asking why I hadn't heard from anyone.

I should've found new counsel after the firm's IT department got involved saying all my emails were "cached in their firm's server". The lawyer didn't receive any of them; but she never reached out to me inquiring about the documents. A short time later, after the first court hearing, I asked the lawyer why she had not listened to an important voice recording I sent her. She said she couldn't open it. There was no reason given, only pushback about me questioning her. Then, when I questioned why needed subpoenas hadn't been prepared or sent, I was told the lawyer was "on vacation".

Then, in April 2020 I was FIRED and thrown to the legal wolves.

I was put on a merry-go-round of local lawyers, telling my story over and over with not one firm wanting to step up and help me. I finally retained another Logan law firm; but I never received important Delaware State Police records that were supposed to have been subpoenaed by this firm, which then caused my dear mother's probate matter & my legal position to be adversely affected. My mother's legacy and her legally-prepared last wishes were dishonored and disrespected on October 21, 2020 by Thomas L. Willmore, an already retired judge who gave all my mother's estate funds to those who were disinherited-by-name in her uncontested will. It was a true Kangaroo Court.

When this firm unceremoniously terminated me as a client I was told "we discounted our fees", for their uncompleted work. I didn't need discounted fees. I'd already paid a retainer. What I needed was important legal work completed. A licensed lawyer was required to protect my mother's legacy and my legal interests in my mother's uncontested will. That's why I hired this firm, to protect her legacy & my legal interests.

My mother suffered in godforsaken Logan, Utah after being improperly taken there by estranged, disinherited offspring. All her estate funds were hijacked under joint tenancy while she was left to suffer isolation and neglect at Legacy House of Logan.

This firm did subpoena records from Legacy House of Logan which reflected isolation, injuries and neglect - so they knew my mother suffered. I myself recently obtained a 2018 Logan City police report reflecting facility staff dissuaded law enforcement from making contact with my sick mother, under my valid welfare check, which contributed to financial theft & physical suffering. An absolute travesty of inhumanity and injustice occurred in Logan, Utah, both before and after my mother died.

I was left without legal counsel by this law firm during the probate matter when I was up against a lying partner in a local firm (who has since retired to chase NFT's and Crypto), and three "interested parties": disinherited-by-name, estranged-for-decades offspring who'd hijacked and absconded with my mother, along with her estate funds, after she was sick with Alzheimer's dementia.

Ultimately I was forced to retain highly competent counsel at Parr Brown Gee & Loveless in Salt Lake City to take back what had been improperly given away at First District Court by an already retired, angry & forgetful, judge who dishonored & disrespected my mother's legally prepared legacy, her uncontested will and me, her loving daughter and sole heir.

RIP Mom <3 You deserved so much better.

Signed, C. Bird,
Loving Daughter & P.R.
Estate of J.G. Pratt

Walking into court i was a apprehensive to say the least; what I  wanted the outcome to be and reality were about to collide. Ken was appointed as my public defender and although I was skittish about possible outcomes,I had little choice, and even less faith they would accept it counter.  Here's what went down, and why I can say Ken is a rarity in the legal profession in the fact that although he looked like he felt my counter was impossible,he complied  and filed the counter for my case.  He then presented my request to the prosecuter, brought up my reasoning for the deal, and what happened next would blow my mind
Case Two started out rocky but Ken saved the day. Although they misdemeanors, I  was facing 5 charges, alone were no big deal, suddenly became a big problem. at and the fines would have been in the thousands. When Ken informed me they accepted the counter, my heart lept, but things were about to get even better. Ken was responsible, although I wasn't present i know he presented the offer with his heart and mind behind it. Remember for me he was an overworked public defender. He could have boiler plated my case and got me out of there quickly and I would have had to accept it. Iknow this from previous experience.  Instead, I walked out of court with a completely different feeling,  one of relief.
If I ever need legal services again, Ken Peterson would be my first choice and number two ain't even close. Ken achieved a deal that had no hope. And that's not the most amazing part of my encounter, Ken did something that no other attorney has ever done and the good fortune with my plea agreement pales in comparison. What he did I've never had happen to me or even witnessed it. Ken could have expressed his belief that the counter was not going to happen, even told me to take the initial deal, and i was wasting time on a dream that wasn't going to happen but he didn't, instead, he listened. The other day in court Ken Peterson not only gained my gratitude and allowed me to relax for the first time in weeks,  he gained my respect as well. Although it's kinda their job to represent their client, apparently most attorneys believe they are representing us. They act on their experience, assessment of case and opposing council, then go before the judge and try to get the best outcome they can. But did they listen to  what the client wanted,  no. Did they spend enough time to understand clients position, no. Now with that in mind did the really represent the clients wishes, if he never asked?
Ken Peterson listens. Most other attorneys do not in my experience.  He took the time to understood my position, made sure I understood what was going on, then Ken Peterson represented me. He didn't go through the motions trying to get rid of me and on to his next case. He represented me like attorneys should represent everyone. How many others can say the same in court that day?

Hello Chris, we’re sorry you were disappointed that we weren’t able to take your matter on at this time. We make a point of accepting only those cases for which we have the capacity to provide appropriate attention and time. If a lawfirm is willing to take any matter on, that's usually not a good sign. As a result, there are periods when we are not taking on new clients or certain types of matters, depending on attorney availability. Nor can we reasonably drop existing client matters to suddenly take your case on.

reviews.text:
I appreciate the opportunity to provide further insight into my experience since the updates to the owner response. It's vital to emphasize that my financial situation is, in fact, quite favorable, and I want to ensure there's no misconception about that. When I initially engaged with this service to explore child support options, I was genuinely hopeful about the prospect of putting my financial matters in order.

As our interactions unfolded, a certain degree of confusion began to creep in. I openly shared all my financial details with Diane Pitcher, delving into various scenarios (These scenarios include with or without litigation). However, upon concluding our discussions, a lingering sense of bewilderment remained. It appeared that the narrative had evolved from our initial conversation, possibly due to misunderstandings on my part. Although I was encouraged to seek an agreement with the other parent before proceeding, the absence of comprehensive information hampered this endeavor, leading to an unsatisfactory outcome.

Of note, the pricing aspect added to my sense of perplexity. Initially, I was quoted a retainer fee of $5,000, but this soon transitioned to $1,500. However, the final cost was communicated as $3,000, and this price alteration came with a requirement for additional charged hours.

It's worth mentioning that the fluctuation in pricing, scenarios, and available options further compounded my sense of confusion. It seemed as though my case hadn't been thoroughly reviewed, necessitating the need to reiterate and clarify key elements of my situation to the attorney and their assistant several times. In a final attempt to gain clarity and resolve the matter, I made one last call. Regrettably, I encountered varying prices and vague explanations that did little to address my doubts and questions effectively.

I acknowledge the promptness of their responses in returning my calls, but my overall comfort with the information provided during the consultation remains unsatisfactory. My limited legal knowledge led me to invest $150 in this endeavor, resorting to online research of state laws to fill the informational gaps I encountered. Surprisingly, I discovered that any state child support services can offer assistance at a significantly reduced cost, eliminating the risk of unwarranted alterations to the custody order.

In essence, when I originally posted my review, my primary concern wasn't affordability but rather the financial viability of these services for my circumstances, particularly after nearly a decade of the existing status quo.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for the feedback. The pricing you list is not accurate reflection of what you were quoted and I'm sorry you were confused. My family law retainer (down payment) is $5,000 that is a general rule. However, I provided advice based on the scenario you provided, which sounded like we could resolve the issue by agreement with other party, and we could draft the petition, stipulation and resulting decrees and try this less expensive, non-litigated approach for financial issues would be $1500. Then that changed for you. Based upon new information you provided (this time on a free consult) the advice changed because it appeared the situation would need to be litigated, so you were given another price. The $5,000 regular retainer (money comes back if you do not use it). However, I decided to lower my usually retainer cost based on your financial situation and the limited scope of representation.
We will make sure in the future that consults are limited to one, not multiple conversations like we had with you to try to help you navigate the situation, and give one price that presumes litigation and not provide discounts due to someone’s personal situation--all to reduce possible confusion when someone's situation changes. We took your phone calls and did not charge you additional consultation fees. We did not need to do this. Thank you for helping us change our policies that can apply to everyone regardless of their circumstances or their financial situation. Thank you for helping us make things less complicated for others in the future. Why you continue to update your comments after more than 6 months is highly perplexing--making me now wonder what on earth you are getting out of this, especially when we called you and offered you your money back.

reviews.text: Brandon Baxter represented an asphalt company I sued for poor workmanship and failure to honor their warranty. I spent months searching for the right attorney for my unemployment appeal and was discouraged by the high consultation fees—some charging hundreds of dollars just to talk. What initially drew me to Loren Peck was his reasonable $50 consultation fee, which was a lifesaver while unemployed. But within minutes of speaking with him, I knew he was the right choice.

Loren is not only incredibly knowledgeable but also genuinely cares. He made me feel comfortable and confident in my case. We prepped over the phone the day before my hearing, and on the day of, we met early to review my former employer’s evidence. To my surprise, they backed out at the last minute, which worked in my favor.

Even so, Loren ensured I presented my side clearly and effectively. He asked sharp follow-up questions, remained professional, and helped me navigate the process stress-free. The judge mentioned a 7-10 day wait for a decision, but just hours later, I got the news—it was in my favor!

I highly recommend Loren to anyone in need of a dedicated, skilled, and compassionate attorney. I’m already considering him for another case. Thank you, Loren, for making me feel heard and fighting for me!

We highly recommend Peck Baxter Watkins & Bailey, LLC. The Attorneys and staff are true professionals and knowledgeable experts. Their team cared about us personally and worked hard to quickly resolve our issue. Attorney Cole Parkinson worked closely with us and provided the answers we needed. He also guided us through the Legal process and ultimately to an equitable conclusion.  We will always use Peck Baxter Watkins & Baily, LLC for any future legal need.

It's a new and pretty brilliant program designed for situations like mine–I had a case that was a bit too complicated to pull off on my own, but not a big enough claim to merit steep legal fees. Loren and his team members would be my first recommendation to anyone who is seeking help with any legal support for an employment issue.

During the lengthy, tumultuous process  Brandon was invaluable. My dad often said, “Just because it’s legal, doesn’t make it right”, and it turns out that “legal justice” can frequently be an oxymoron. However, Brandon Baxter (and his associates Jodie Cranney and Kristie Cook) are truly kind, and made it all so much easier.  Contacting Brandon was the best decision in my pain-ridden saga.  I would highly recommend him to anyone (in fact, I already have). He’s a great attorney and an even better human being.

I had the wonderful opportunity to meet and work with Lauren Peck today. I was surprised by his caring treatment considering the fact that my case was small and would do little for his business. Not only was he caring, he was honest as well. He was up-front with my legal options and didn’t sugar coat anything but still offered options to help. If I ever need legal counsel again, I’m coming back and I suggest anyone who reads this do the same.

Recently I was engaged in a case with an insurance company.  Choosing the right attorney is vital to securing a successful outcome, and I couldn’t have found a better attorney than Shaun Peck of Peck Hadfield Baxter & Moore.  As a consummate professional, Shaun paid attention to every detail so that he could provide sound advice.  His easy-going nature allowed him to develop rapport quickly with me, expert witnesses, arbitrators, mediators, and other attorneys, including opposing counsel.  I discovered that his knack for developing positive relationships makes him well respected by all.  His modest confidence in himself and in my case instilled in me an understanding of his competence, and I trusted him completely as he took me through each step of my case.  Because Shaun is well connected, he was able to find a competent expert to testify in my case.  In addition, I appreciate his critical thinking skills—during depositions, he was able to turn opposing expert testimony to my benefit by asking the right questions.  He and Tonya, his capable paralegal, were easily accessible by phone and email to answer any questions I had.  I found Shaun to be honest, ethical, and forthright; and his suggested courses of action were definitely on point.  Furthermore, he was respectful of me and empathetic towards how my case would affect me emotionally and mentally.  He was also cognizant of how the legal process of my particular case could influence significant relationships in my life; and he did everything in his power to minimize the impact.  For these reasons I highly recommend Shaun Peck as a competent and trustworthy attorney.

We have worked with this office on several issues, from estate planning to adoption and legal issues as well.  This office is highly knowledgeable and certainly the most friendly folks you could find.  We are MORE than pleased with their services.

I cannot recommend Michael D. Zilles highly enough. I was in court for a ticket related to a lapse in insurance and felt completely overwhelmed. I had already spoken to the prosecutor on my own and was told there was nothing that could be done — it looked like I would have to plead guilty, get SR-22 insurance, and possibly risk losing my license.

Then I saw Mr. Zilles in court helping another client, and something about the way he carried himself gave me new hope. Even though he was clearly there for someone else, I took a chance and asked if he was an attorney and explained my situation. He was incredibly kind and didn’t seem bothered at all by my interruption. He listened, reassured me, and told me he could likely get me a better outcome — and he absolutely did.

He handled everything from that point on, kept me informed, and made the process so much easier. When we returned to court, my case was one of the first called, and the ticket was dismissed immediately. I didn’t have to wait around, and I didn’t have to do a bunch of legal legwork either — he made it seamless. He was professional, responsive, and genuinely compassionate throughout.

If you’re feeling lost or overwhelmed like I was, Michael D. Zilles is exactly the person you want by your side!

I called Kent Hansen's office in Logan to ask for assistance with my parents' trust since I am now the trustee and need to settle the trust. I had previously called the attorney who created the trust as well as another firm in Logan but, after waiting for several weeks, I received no response from either of them.

I was pleased and relieved that Maria could schedule a 15 minute consultation within a few days of my call. Mr. Hansen called at the appointed time and provided some initial guidance. We scheduled another call a few days later to discuss the trust.

At the next meeting, it was obvious that he had read the trust and was familiar with the provisions. During our discussion, he answered all of my questions and provided the guidance I needed. He also reviewed and corrected two documents that were required for me to act in the capacity of the trustee.

All of this was done over the phone or in a Zoom call since I am out of the state. I am extremely pleased with the assistance that Mr. Hansen provided. He is very professional, understanding, knowledgeable, and friendly. I highly recommend him.
